opts: Let the puck slide out and regroup after
Skate to the puck and keep it in the zone
Back up to the blue line to give yourself room
Skate quickly to the front of your own net
Crash the net hard right away and look for a quick rebound chance
Put pressure on the D so they can't make an easy breakout pass
Wait at your own blue line until your linemates catch up first
Skate back to the red line and regroup with your teammates
Stay high and wait for a long stretch pass breakout
Turn around and skate back toward your own zone
Stop where you are and call hard for a pass
Stand still and watch how the play develops next
Give them lots of space so they cannot skate past you
A steady controlled gap — not too much, not too little
No space — close on them as fast as you can skate
It doesn't really matter as long as you stay with them
Into the corner to help win the puck battle
Up the boards — give the D an outlet
Into the slot to screen the other goalie
All the way to the far blue line
Try to score a shorthanded goal on every clearing chance you get
Keep the puck away from dangerous areas and get it out of your zone
Collapse into a tight box and block every single shot lane close
Pressure the puck carrier hard every single time they touch the puck
Go down into the corner and help your teammate battle for it
On the right side near the net, ready for a cross-ice pass
At the blue line ready to grab a loose puck off the wall
Heading to the bench so your line can change off the ice
Shoot right away before the defenders can close on you
Look up and read what's happening — find the best play
Skate into the corner and let the winger pick it up
Pass it backward to your defenseman at the point
Pass right away since on a 2-on-1 the D cannot cover both players
Drive wide to force the defender to fully commit first, then decide
Shoot from where you are right now before the defender can react
Pull up and regroup with your line at the offensive blue line first
Yes — pinch down right away and help your winger battle hard for the puck
No — hold the blue line. If your winger loses the puck you'll be caught deep.
Go to the front of the net and crash the crease for a rebound chance there
Skate behind the net to support your winger along the goal line down below there
Hold onto the puck and try to fight through all the pressure by yourself
Make a quick D-to-D pass to your partner — they're open and you're not
Fire the puck hard through the traffic right on net from the blue line
Chip the puck back down into the corner and skate in to chase it
Hold the puck and wait for the slot traffic to clear out first
Shoot immediately — traffic in front means screens and rebounds
Pass cross-ice to the other point and reset the whole play
Skate into the slot yourself and try to beat the goalie in tight
Race into the corner and join the battle to win the puck using numbers down low
Stay high between the two defensemen — cut off passes and take away the middle
Go straight to the net-front and be a screen for any point shot from above
Back up to the blue line and wait to support from up top if the puck comes
Go straight at the D at full speed to force a rushed play
Cut off the pass to the left — force the D to go right instead
Stop in the middle of the ice to block a shot going the other way
Drop back to the blue line and let your D make the next play
Drive hard straight to the net and try to finish off a rebound
Peel off and go to the weak side — create a triangle of options
Pull up at the blue line and wait for your wingers to join up
Follow the puck carrier into the corner to support the battle down low
Skate straight at them at full speed and try to split the three defenders
Delay — use a D-to-D pass or drop pass to find a lane that opens up
Dump the puck deep into their zone and chase it down hard together
Regroup in your own zone and come back up the exact same side again
Try to power through the defender and muscle hard through to the net
Use a quick spin move or chip the puck off the boards to your winger
Slide a pass between the defender's legs to a teammate in the slot
Fall down against the boards and try to draw a tripping call
Try to carry the puck out to the left side fast
Spin to the right and find your open defenseman
Stop and wait for the forechecker to commit first
Shoot the puck down the ice and change lines
Hold the puck and fight straight through the pressure while protecting it with your body
Move the puck quickly before the pressure arrives — pass to the half-wall or the other side
Shoot it straight through the pressure no matter where the lane is — any shot on net is good
Back the puck up toward center ice so you can regroup and make a new zone entry later
Get out of the goalie's line of sight so they can track the shot
Screen the goalie and be ready to tip or deflect the shot
Skate to the corner and be ready to grab a rebound
Drop to the half-wall as an outlet for a pass out of pressure
Pressure every single pass the second it leaves their stick to force mistakes
When they slow down or seem to be setting up a shot — not on every pass
Every time the puck goes near the boards or along the wall in close
Only when the puck gets up to the point defenseman at the top
Try to deke past both defenders and work your way to the net by yourself
Protect the puck along the boards and wait for a teammate to support
Pass blindly into the slot and hope a teammate is there to get it cleanly
Shoot from right where you are and look for your own rebound chance
Just holding the puck as long as possible to keep it away from the other team's sticks
A gap or opening — a shot lane, a pass to the slot, or a player breaking to the net
Confusion by passing the puck around as fast as they can to every single teammate out there
A penalty call by trying to draw contact from the tight defenders around the crease
Try to power through and hold the puck on the boards yourself
Bank a quick pass off the boards to your half-wall winger
Spin toward the middle ice and try to drive hard to the net
Shoot the puck on net from down low in the corner area
Hold the puck and wait for a teammate to call for it to come out
Move the puck quickly — first open option up the wall or through
Rim it around the boards right away without even looking up
Shoot it down the ice for an icing whistle and take the faceoff
Force a pass across to your left winger even though the defender is right on top of them
Pass to the right winger — they're open and the forechecker has committed to that side
Carry the puck out of the zone yourself and try to beat the forechecker on your right side
Rim the puck hard around the boards and hope it reaches your winger up the wall clean
Pass it across to your teammate exactly like you planned before
Shoot — the goalie has moved and there's a lane on your side
Pass the puck back the other way and reset the whole play
Pull up and regroup with your teammate at the blue line
Skate full speed at the puck carrier and try to poke the puck away from behind
Skate back hard on the weak side — get between the second attacker and the net
Go straight to the net to support your goalie and block any open shot lanes
Peel off to the bench and get a quick line change on the fly for fresh legs
Both defensemen shift over to the left side to cover the two attackers and match up with numbers on the overloaded side
One D takes the two on the left, the other holds the middle and takes the weak-side attacker — both D communicate
Both D shift over to the weak right side and defend the back door where the cross-ice pass is almost always coming in
One D skates behind the net and pre-rotates to take away any wraparound plays before they can develop from down low
